summary:
SPIRITUAL AWAKENING AND MANIFESTATIONS: Brandon expresses a deep desire for a departure from status-quo Christianity, advocating for an unusual and radical faith. He emphasizes the importance of abiding in God's word and building a foundation of love, specifically referencing 1 Corinthians 13. By doing so, he believes believers can unlock profound spiritual manifestations, healings, and angelic visitations. Brandon stresses that believers should expect the impossible, arguing that a strong expectation is what ultimately brings about supernatural manifestations in daily life and church services.

WALKING IN LOVE AND EVANGELISM: Brandon recounts a challenging personal situation involving severe property damage to his septic tank and home flooding. Instead of reacting with anger, he felt led by the Holy Spirit to respond with love and compassion toward the worker responsible. This act of grace opened the door for Brandon to evangelize to the man, warning him about the end times, the Antichrist, and impending natural disasters like earthquakes in Oregon. He uses this experience to illustrate that passing the enemy's fleshly tests through love and maintaining peace are crucial for displaying true Christian fruit and witnessing to the lost.

PROSPERITY AND REJECTING POVERTY: Brandon fiercely advocates for the financial and material blessing of believers, explicitly rejecting the concept of a vow of poverty. Drawing inspiration from figures like Brother Hagin and John G. Lake, he argues that Christians represent the King and should expect excellent provisions, such as new cars, houses, and financial abundance. He mentions observing high-end vehicles in Tulsa and hoping for cryptocurrency to surge, framing these as part of a great wealth transfer for the righteous. Brandon encourages his audience to celebrate the blessings of others and believe for extraordinary provisions, emphasizing that God wants His children to eat the good of the land.

COMMUNION AND INTERCESSION: Brandon details his practice of taking daily communion, not as a religious sacrament, but as an intimate, relational covenant that allows him to enter the spiritual realm and sit at the feet of God. He pleads the blood of Jesus over his family, finances, and possessions, aggressively rebuking the devourer and demanding the return of stolen blessings. This foundational act of faith grounds his spiritual authority, allowing him to confidently pray for the healing, marital restoration, and deliverance of others without being moved by physical symptoms or worldly circumstances.

VISION OF THE DRY BONES AND COMING REVIVAL: Brandon shares a profound and extended prophetic vision heavily inspired by Ezekiel 37, where he witnesses a vast valley filled with dry bones representing spiritually dead and lukewarm Christians. In the vision, Satan is seen laughing at the state of the church, but God commands Brandon to prophesy life into the bones. He describes a massive supernatural event where a dam breaks, and a mighty flood of the Holy Ghost combining the former and latter rains washes over the valley. The bones are miraculously restored with flesh, tendons, and sparkling spiritual garments, symbolizing a monumental end-times revival, a global awakening, and the ultimate defeat of the enemy's plans.

OVERCOMING THE VICTIM MENTALITY: Brandon concludes his spiritual message by urging believers to completely abandon a victim mentality and step out of their metaphorical caves of bondage. He explains that many Christians have become so accustomed to hardship and spiritual attacks that they expect defeat, effectively chaining themselves inside open cages. By changing their mindset and embracing the victory already won by Christ, believers can step into a higher level of faith, endure the threshing floor, and produce the pure oil of the Holy Spirit necessary for the coming revival.
rawData: [May 25, 2025]-[A valley of bones vision - can these bones live - a vision from the Lord in prayer today]-[https://youtu.be/tbUShTZrtiA]-[Hey, good morning, family. Praise the Lord. Thank you for joining us today. Well, I just want to thank every single one of you again. Thank you for coming. Every single time we do a video, you come out and you pray. You take things seriously. This is where I've been. I told the Lord, I said, "I don't want to be where I'm at today this way tomorrow. I'm tired of being just the status quo. I'm tired of the status quo. I'm tired of Christianity as usual. I'm ready for Christianity unusual." This is why when you get fed up being the way you are, that's when you'll start to see change. I want change. Diana and I, Braden, Megan, all of us. I want change in my daughter. I want change in my family. I want change, expecting God to do something big in your life. And I said, "Lord, I want the revival. I want healings, demonstrations, and manifestations of your glory in these last days. You said we would walk in this. Where's the kink in the hose?" And the Bible says, "If ye abide in me and my words remain in you, you shall ask what you will." But it's about remaining in this word and getting this word's foundation in your life, the love walk, going back to 1 Corinthians 13 and meditating on what He said. Getting that word, the love, because the glory of what He said, Brandon—the glory of those big silos of that honey glow—was connected to love, getting a love foundation in your life from 1 Corinthians 13, meditating on that, and getting that word of what the Bible says about love taking no account of a suffered wrong when people do you wrong and all these things, but also believing God that He's going to do it. Having an expectation for manifestations, because expectation brings the manifestation. I believe that God is going to show up in this service tomorrow for your churches, or for wherever you have a church or prayer group. I expect for us to get something radical from the Holy Spirit today. I do not want to go away and have another lukewarm service that everybody kind of feels was okay, whatever, bypassing another week. I know from glory to glory He's changing me. I want that so bad. The only thing that's ever going to change you is having a foundation of this word down inside of your heart, changing you from the inside out. That's the only thing. And that's what Brother Hagin—he drove it home. That's all he ever talked about: "Faith cometh by hearing, hearing by the word of God," you know. And R.W. Schambach, all the greats. I said, "My goodness, Lord, help me. If I'm a closer, I don't want to let these guys down. I don't want to let you down. I want to finish strong. Lord, let us do something big for you because the word is working mightily in me. The word is working mightily in me. Do you believe it? Do you believe it?" But then He said to me when I was driving around today, He said, "Brandon, I want you to start to believe for manifestations of angelic encounters—not flaky stuff, but truly changing you from the inside out—the word, believing for that. But not only that, believing for demonstrations of the Holy Spirit, manifestations of the Holy Spirit, and angelic visitations in your services every day, Lord, by love." I get this love like He said, love from 1 Corinthians 13. Get in that love. Okay, so if I'm walking in love, and you are love, and you're inside of me, and I'm inside with you, Lord, and we take this communion every day, and we're expecting for you to show up in the mall, showing up in these circumstances. There was a guy, when I had all these things going on with our house, I told you the guy hit our septic, and it was like thousands of dollars to get this septic fixed. But when he was here, my first reaction was just to lose it, because he just did all this, right? And he wasn't going to fix it because I didn't tell him where the septic was. So it was, you know, and he said, "I'll go in half." But the Holy Spirit said, "Walk in love. Walk in love. Walk in love. Don't lose it on this man. There's more to this than what meets the eye." And so, to make a long story short, another guy came, and he was a Hispanic man, and the Lord said to me, "He does not know me." Witness to him, impart love into him, and talk to him about the word. You could feel instantaneously the devil inside of him starts talking out. "Are you smoking dope? Are you doing this stuff?" because you're talking about the Holy Ghost. You're talking about the end times, "Jesus is coming. The end times are here." I'm just bold about my faith, folks. I'm not going to ever just back down. And if the Holy Spirit comes on me, you're going to see a radical evangelist right there. And that's what I do. And so I start telling him about the Antichrist and about earthquakes. This is what's coming. Oregon is fixing to have a major earthquake. This is stuff because I know God puts these signs. Signs will follow them that believe. Signs will follow them that believe; He said they would. And God gives us these things to be a light in the darkness to this world, not to be flaky. What makes us stand out? It's the signs. It's the wonders. It's whenever we touch somebody, and they are lost as a goose in the fog, and you lay hands on them, and the power of God sets them free, and they go from glory to glory. There was something to that. It's not just watered-down, lukewarm religion that you're getting from stale preachers behind the pulpit, but you're teaching people that Jesus Christ is coming and they need to repent because if they don't, they're going to hell. He came to set the captives free. He told us to do the Great Commission: to go into all the world, lay hands on the sick, and they would recover, cast out devils, raise the dead, do all the things that He said we could do. Why aren't we seeing it? Because we're not believing it. I want to be a believing believer. And when I saw that man, the Lord told me, "If he dies tonight, Brandon, he's going to hell." I had compassion inside of me. I wanted to—it would look flaky—but bend down on my knees right then and grab his ankle and say, "If you die tonight, sir, the Lord told me you're going to hell. I don't know what it is with you, but God's calling you. God's calling you. God's calling your son that's standing next to you." And I feel to intercede and just weep on behalf of your soul because you don't realize how close you are to stepping over. I don't know what it was about that man, but he came to my house, and I figured he's here for a reason. It's not a coincidence. "Yeah, you messed up my septic. Yeah, you did all this stuff, and I'm showing you love because my flesh wants to just be really ugly right now. What do you do for a living, sir?" "I'm a preacher." "I could be a real jerk right now. I really could because of what you did, but I'm going to show you love, and I'm going to pay for it. I'm going to pay for it. It doesn't cost you anything. It was an accident." But you have the opportunity right then to show the love of God to somebody, and you can totally lose it because thousands of dollars is a lot, or you can love on somebody because they made a mistake. Yeah, it was a costly one, but my point is that's the love that He's talking about in 1 Corinthians 13 right now. Not all things are always easy, especially when you're dealing with the world and they mess up your yard and they do all the things that they do. And you know what? The one guy came back and made it all right. He went out and cleaned up the stuff, and he apologized because it was bad. I'm just telling y'all, it was bad. A lot of things. My house is leaking water clear into my bedroom. There's a lot going on this last week. Then we made the video last week. Wednesday evening, we got home from church, did all that, got so excited. Thursday morning, we woke up. We're going to make another video for you all, and Braden's computer has completely crashed. All of the software, all of it completely gone, wiped out. It's like we got hacked in the middle of the night. Literally, the computer won't start, won't do anything. And he goes, 'Daddy, something bad has happened to the computer that we got blessed with. It's not working at all.' And I said, 'What happened?' He goes, 'It won't even boot up. There's nothing. It won't even do anything.' It was completely frozen. Everything about this after we made that video about the energy and everything. So, I figured we got hacked. Something happened in the middle of the night. I still don't know what happened. So, he had to reboot it back up. He's watching YouTube videos. All these little pricks because I know I'm on the right road and I'm going somewhere with this. You know you're on the right road when you're getting these little pricks because the enemy is ticked off because I'm putting a kink in his hose and he doesn't like it because I'm teaching a lot of you. You know how many people email every day, 'You're teaching me to pray. You're teaching me how to live by faith. I was suicidal. My marriage has been restored.' How many people have gotten their lives changed by watching these videos? I'm humbled. So many people said, 'I never knew how to pray in the Holy Ghost and baptism. I got filled with the Holy Ghost and fire watching these videos and I pray with you every single day.' How many people said, 'I was lukewarm and you turned me around? I watch you instead of pastors now because I get more out of your sermons.' I said, 'My God, what an honor that you'd come to listen to me.' But after you get all these little pricks and things, and Braden finally, it was the grace of God watching a YouTube video that we were able to—because he said, 'Daddy, we're going to have to buy a new computer. This computer's gone.' And watching a YouTube video, he fixed it. Thank God. Took him all day long Thursday. All day long, we didn't have it. And I told him, I said, 'Whatever happens happens, buddy. We'll make it work.' Whenever the enemy comes in and he tries to test you to see if he's getting to you by how you react. How are you reacting to the septic tank getting run over, the water line, and the water flooding my bathroom and ruining it, and I had to go and fix all that, the boards falling down from the storm? All these little things. The enemy tried to get you all in your thoughts, but it all goes back to 1 Corinthians 13. Will you walk in love? Will you allow your fruit to shine to men? And whenever the devil's trying to get your joy with all these little things and it all adds up, like I said, thousands, and my flesh, like I said, I was—wow. Okay. So, whenever all that's coming on you and all that's going on and you have the opportunity to walk in love, right? Walk in love. The Bible says, 'Take no thought, saying.' Take no thought, saying. Why? Because out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks. And the devil cannot read your thoughts. The devil can't go around looking, 'Am I getting to him?' So the only way he can know if he's getting to you is by how you're responding on the outside in the natural. Because the devil's a flesh devil. He doesn't get anything by the spirit of God. He has to move by the flesh. And he's not omnipresent. He is either with you or whatever. He's not like the Holy Spirit, right? So, his demons are looking to go back and report to him, 'This worked and this didn't work.' But when you show the love of God, you pass the test. That's what I'm trying to tell y'all. When you get cut off on the road, when you do these things and people treat you badly at the restaurant—you might have a bad server at the restaurant. She might give you bad service and you show love and say, 'I'll give her a good tip anyway. She doesn't deserve it.' And you might want to just tell her boss, 'She's the most lousy server I've ever had. She didn't get my drinks refilled. She didn't give me good service the whole time. I'm telling you what, I want to let this girl have a piece of my mind,' you know, but you go and you say, 'Honey, I know this may not be your calling, but I'm going to bless you anyway and show you love,' you know, 20 bucks or whatever. I don't know, that's a big tip, I'm just saying you give them a big tip. They may say, 'Hey, I can't believe how I treated you' or whatever. I don't know, I just try to show God's love until the point of where it's painful to my flesh because there's something more about it in the glory, is what I'm trying to tell you all. Where is the glory? Where's the outpouring of the spirit of God? Like we've never seen it before. The Lord told me, he said, 'Brandon, Jesus did not do any mighty works until somebody put a demand on it.' Look at what he did with the water turning into wine. Mary put a demand on that gift inside of him. But it wasn't time yet. 30 years, nothing, and then somebody put a demand on it and caused that gift to flourish. And that's why I feel like a lot of times, maybe that's where we're at. Maybe it wasn't season yet. But the Lord said it's time to put a demand on what is rightfully ours. When I was driving around today, he said it's time to put a demand on it. It's time for you to expect, because I was driving under a bridge. I can't even tell you where I was. I was on Memorial Drive in Tulsa, Oklahoma. And I was going under the bridge because I was looking at the Range Rovers, I was looking at the Cadillac dealership, I was believing big. Looking at those cars and going, 'Look at those cars. That's the wealth of the sinner laid up for the righteous. Look at those brand new Escalades, look at those brand new Range Rovers, look at those brand new cars. Look at what the world has.' And yes, they have it, but greater is he that is in me. Yes. He said he would provide, the wealth of the sinners is laid up for the righteous. I believe it. It's a wealth transfer time. Some people don't want to agree with me on that. That's fine, stay where you are, but don't criticize me for believing big. I was driving underneath the bridge there, and the Lord started talking to me about the angels, and he said, 'Brandon, remember I told you as you're passing under this bridge to believe for angelic visitation in these services? Believe for the angels to work with you to bring the body parts.' I told you when we got in prayer, if an octopus can get a new limb and grow back new limbs, if a crab can do it and they're sea-dwelling animals, how much more are you able to believe for a new arm or a new leg or new eyes? If they can grow back stuff, why can't we? We're created in his image. We're created to be the head over all these things. He said to take dominion and authority over these animals. How much more do we have the ability to grow back something? 'Well, I don't believe that. We've never seen it.' It's time to believe for the impossible. It's time to believe. God forbid. Are we in the last days or not? Are we going to see signs will follow them that believe? I'm so done with being a Christian that's just barely, you know, 'Well, we see it, well, we may not.' Every once in a while, it's whatever, man. I'm believing for it every day. Let's believe for God to do something every day. Believe God for finances, like, we need to look nice for him. We represent the King. He's corrected me about my clothes. He's corrected me about a lot of things, saying, "Brandon, you represent me. You need to look nice for me. When you go into public, you need to look nice. Don't wear your scrubby stuff out and always have holes. Look nice for me. Look nice." Why? Because John G. Lake used to look in the mirror. He put on a white suit, a white tie, and all these white clothes. And he looked and said, "God dwells on the inside of this man, and I represent him. I represent the King of Kings and the Lord of Lords. And he dwells on the inside of this man." So I started thinking about what John G. Lake did, and he had an appearance of excellence. His car was clean. Things looked nice. When you represent the King, you look nice. You clean yourself up. You may not have a dime to your name, but a bar of soap only costs so much. And you look nice with a bar of soap. You don't have to look scruffy and dirty, with oily hair. You can have an appearance of cleanliness, too, because cleanliness is godliness. My mom would always say, "Cleanliness is godliness." I say, "Well, you don't have to stink with B.O.. You can have a bar of soap, scrub your armpits down, and make yourself smell nice." I mean, there's a point of appearance, an appearance of excellence, showing up and believing God for more. Believing God for more. Well, this guy said, "I believe in God. Do you think the Lord wants me to have a new car, brother?" That's what I'm talking about, driving around looking at that. That's when I heard Brother Hagin teaching that today. I said, "Let's go look at the Cadillacs. Let's go look at the Range Rovers. Let's go look for more." Because he said, "Is it wrong to have a brand new car? Is it wrong to not be buying used all the time?" And he said, "You're representing the King. You're representing the King. And if you, being evil, give good gifts to your kids, how much more does your Heavenly Father give good gifts to those that love him and are hearing him? How much more?" So I started thinking, well, why not believe for bigger? Why not believe God for more? Why not believe God for a newer house? Why not believe God for this stuff? I believed for this house that I'm living in right now, and it's time. And the Lord said, "Brandon, look what I gave you this time last year. Look how I took care of you this time last year." I was sitting on my back patio. I lived in a shotgun house in Guthrie, Oklahoma. Bad mice coming in. We had rodents. We had snakes coming up through the floorboards. Our house had mold so bad we were falling through the foundation of the floor. Lived there for 22 years. 22 years my kids, all of them born in there, lived there all those years, being a janitor at the church, making nothing, believing God, believing God. And the Lord said, "Stay here. Take care of your grandma." I took care of my grandmother all that time. I complained—I'm not going to lie to y'all, I did complain to the Lord. "Lord, I need something better. My family—the mold and mildew smell in here is so bad, all the things that we dealt with for so many years." But God came through and got us this house we're living in now. 23 years. And people say, "Well, Brandon, your house is nice now." But you don't know what I came from. You don't know what God did. I never get mad at somebody's blessing whenever you see what they have and where they are, because you don't know what seeds they sowed to get what they've got. I rejoice whenever I see a preacher have more. I rejoice whenever I see my brother and sister get a brand new car, because you don't know what seeds they sacrificed to get what they got. You don't know what prayers they prayed. You don't know what they gave. Do we not believe that the Bible is true, that he gives back 30, 60, and a hundredfold? Why do we get mad when we see somebody get blessed? If the Bible is true and he gives back 30, 60, and a hundredfold, why aren't more of us walking in increase? Is it because we are ashamed of what he's given us? We don't want to—"Oh, well, we kind of live down and out. We just got to barely get by." "I took the vow of poverty." I'm going to tell you this: you're a fool. I do not want to receive a vow of poverty. I'm not taking no vow of poverty. If the Bible says that the windows of heaven are open over us and he poured out a blessing that we don't have room enough to receive it all, how much more can we walk in it, to not walk by just barely? That's why I'm believing God for crypto to take off like a rocket. I talked to a dear sister. She lives in Florida, and she said she's believing for big, Brandon, and she's wanting to retire. She had all these big desires of believing God to have exceeding. She works for the TSA and all this stuff. She told me, she said, "I work for the TSA, worked there for like twenty-some, 30 years or something like that, but I'm believing God to do something big." And I thought, "You inspire me. You inspire me." She is a single woman saying she's inspired to believe God for bigger. I come across you all in life, and we are changed by you all. We are changed by what you're believing for. You inspire me to believe bigger. I thought, "Yeah, why not? The sky is the limit. We limit ourselves by what we think." So that's what I had for you all today. I want you to believe for bigger. And whenever you see the enemy try and attack you, whenever he tries to come against you, take no thought saying. I'm not going to take thoughts of what I'm feeling and lose my peace in all of this. I'm not going to take a thought saying and let him see it. The enemy is not going to see me sweat in all the drama. Do you know God is good, folks? God is good, and he is a rewarder of those that diligently seek him. The Bible says the willing and the obedient will eat the good of the land. And I'm telling you, it's time for the willing and the obedient. I've been willing and I've been obedient to eat the good of the land. It's time to eat the good of the land. Are you willing to eat the good of the land? If you've been willing and obedient, you've been faithful tithing, you've been faithful giving, it's time for the willing and obedient to eat the good of the land. But it's not just about money; it's about peace that passes all understanding. It's about joy unspeakable and full of glory. It's about your marriage being restored. It's about healing in your body. It's about all these things that he said that he would provide for us if we believe him for it. So, we're going to believe God today. That's where I'm at. I'm not going to be a doubter. I'm a believer. I'm an overcomer. I live on the victory side. Do you believe that today? Do you believe you live on the victory side? Well, "We ain't never seen victory before. We're just barely holding the fort." I'm just going to say it out my mouth: I have what the Bible says I can have. I can do what the Bible says I can do. I refuse to allow the enemy to keep coming and stealing and taking my stuff. I call back double what you stole. He rebukes the devourer for my sake. Do you believe that? I want to be a believing believer. I believe I'm a believing believer. So, let's believe God today. Like I was believing for the Hispanic man that came over to my house, I believe that there are seeds that are planted. Some people plant, some people water, and some bring forth the increase. Not everybody's always going to bring forth all the increase. Some people plant, some people water, and some people bring forth the increase. But I believe I planted and maybe even watered. And that's what you have to keep doing is planting, watering, but it's time for the harvesters. For the harvest is plentiful and the laborers are few. Right now, there's more people on this planet combined than what there has been in all the generations. Think about 8 billion, 9 billion people. 9 billion at one time. And yet, in any generation before, we've never had that kind of population ever. Think about that. 9 billion people basically. That's a lot of people. Folks say, "Whoa." But it's time to believe big. Let's take communion today and we're going to intercede. I'm going to pray and we're going to intercede today for whatever—well, see what he has for us. We'll dip in the river together and we're going to intercede and we're going to pray for supernatural impartation for him to show us that which is to come. That we would see things, as being watchmen. That's what I'm trying to say. That we'll be watchmen today, and we're going to see on the wall. If there's anything to see, he'll show us. Father, we lift up this body before you. And I never enter into that place of spiritual intercession without taking communion first. You say, "Why do you take communion so much?" Because I always enter in by the blood of the lamb, by the word of my testimony. I enter in by the blood. I don't go sit on his throne without taking communion first. Never. I always go before him with communion and say, "Father, I'm here. Father, I'm sitting at your feet today. I draw near to you. But the only way I'm able to is by this covenant. By this covenant. And this represents you. I do it in an act of faith. I do it by an act of faith. This is my faith. Faith without works is dead. This is my faith working. This is what I do. Father, I believe you. You're the Son of God. I believe you died and you rose again on the third day. I believe that you dwell on the inside of me. That's why I take communion all the time because it represents something to me. Does it make sense? I don't do it for religious purposes. This isn't religious to me. Well, I'm taking sacraments. No, I'm doing it because it's relationship to me. I don't try to be religious when I pray. I'm just real when I pray. You guys, Braden, I'm the most real person when it comes to the spiritual realm than anybody because I know I have nothing to hide. He can see it all. He knows everything, all my thoughts. He knows all my failures. He knows everything about me. And there's nothing I can do other than to come to him just as I am and just say, "Listen, I am flawed without you. I need you." And in this, you fill me. You make me whole. You make me well. You make me everything that I need, you have. And there's nothing in this life I can do apart from you. And this represents you, Holy Communion. So, Father, we lift up this body before you. Now, we thank you, Lord, for all that you've done and all that you've yet to do in our lives. Father, I know it's finished. I know that you said we're well. We're healed. We're whole. We're all these things that you said we could do, we can do because your body, your blood was sacrificed for us. I thank you, Father. I thank you, Lord, for whom the Son has set free. And I take this body in the name of Jesus. And I thank you, Father, that I'm healed, I'm whole, I'm well. I have the mind of Christ. Clear thoughts, no brain fog, no nothing. I am healed, whole and well. I am delivered. I am set free. I thank you, Lord. All that I need. Everything that you've ever done is done. You said it is finished. And we take this body in the name of Jesus. You believe that today? Do you believe that he did it all for you today? Yes. We believe the blood set us free. We confess our sins, Lord. We messed up. We've fallen short of the glory. But we are all okay because of this blood. We're right through your blood. The blood made us whole. The blood made us well. Thank you, Father, for the blood. Thank you for the blood, Lord. Thank you, Lord, that you redeemed me by the blood. Thank you, Lord, that you set me free. Thank you, Lord, for whom the Son sets free is free indeed. I plead the blood of Jesus over my children. I plead the blood of Jesus over my wife. I plead the blood of Jesus over me, my marriage, my finances, my house, my car. Father, thank you, Lord, that you rebuke the devourer for my sake today. The devourer has been found and he has to pay back double what he stole in Jesus' name. I'm calling it in right now in the name of Jesus. Everything the devil stole, I call it in in the name of Jesus. I say you rebuke the devourer, Father, and I stand on what you said I can have. And I will do what the Bible says I can do. And I take back what you stole from me, devil, in Jesus' name. Father, I thank you for the blood. I sit at your feet, Father, because of that blood. It's on that mercy seat. "Hey, Jesus, how are you doing today?" That's what I say. "Oh, Jesus, I thank you for all you've done for me." Thank you, Jesus. I want you to be real to me today. Be real to me, Holy Spirit, today. It's not something in my imagination; it's something real. I thank you, Father, that you're real to me today. That you said I could do it. You said I could have it. And I believe for the impossible. I believe, Father, that what they say is impossible is possible through you. That all things are possible to him who believes.
